examples.subject: Re: Contact Form
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi Brad,

Thank you for providing me with this information.

Please find the map of the Verizon towers in your area; your location will be circled.

You can view this map here.

Looks like you have 2 high-frequency towers in your area. The tower to your south is the one serving your area currently; however, both would be viable if using a directional antenna. Please keep these directions in mind when installing an antenna system.

In your case, I would recommend the QuadPro, as it is compatible with your gateway.

As for what side of the building would be best, you can test both the north and south sides and install the antenna on the side that delivers the best performance. 

Regarding latency, latency is primarily controlled by distance from the tower and the gateway hardware; unfortunately, an antenna can't influence this.

Please let me know what your thoughts are in terms of cable runs. Keep in mind the cabling will be in increments of 10-ft and 20-ft. As soon as I have that information, I can create a quote for you.

Let me know your thoughts on the recommendation.

Best,

sopMatches.title: Pipedrive
sopMatches.excerpt:
Pipedrive

PipeDrive is our central CRM (customer relationship management) tool. As a sales team, it's where we keep information about our customers and deals, where we track our activities and record our sales.

Following a couple of key best practices ensures that we're working collaboratively and that we never need to "dig up" crucial information.

Note taking

As the team continues to grow and customers speak with more of us about any given deal, let's reinforce the habit of leaving great notes in PipeDrive to make picking up where someone else left off as easy as possible.

Here are some

sopMatches.excerpt:
Documentation Best Practices

As our team continues to grow and customers speak with more of us about any given deal, it's crucial that we leave great notes in PipeDrive and DEAR to make picking up where someone else left off as easy as possible. 

Mission

Leaving great notes makes it easier for you, your team mates and other departments to understand what's going on and how best to assist a customer quickly. Leaving poor notes or no notes at all has the exact opposite effect.

Good notes streamline our team work within and across departments.

examples.subject: Re: Contact Form
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi Brad,

Thank you so much for reaching out,

I'm Ruan from the Sales and Support team at Waveform, and I'd be more than happy to assist you in any way I can.

I want to make sure that we recommend the best solution for your needs. Before we proceed with a recommendation, there are a few general questions that will help us move on to the next steps that I’ve listed below:

What is the address where the setup will be done? (For reviewing cell tower maps.)

What are your current data speeds, with and without the antenna?

How would you describe your internet usage? (e.g., streaming, working from home, gaming, etc.)

Regarding the 75-ft cable, I would definitely not recommend having such a long cable run. Signal degrades over distance in the cable. I would recommend not exceeding 30 ft of cable from the antenna to the gateway. The loss would be of such a nature that it might render the antenna ineffective at 75-ft.

If you have any questions or concerns, please let me know!

Hope you have a lovely day.

Best,

sopMatches.excerpt:
Case study requests

Case studies are invaluable resources that we use to reach new markets, prove our expertise, and showcase our excellent work.

Getting the info we need from clients can sometimes be daunting, so here's how to go about that.

If you need assistance or have any questions, feel free to reach out to Marcus.

Requesting a case study:

It's important to be open and upfront, and prioritize the value it holds for them and other people like them.

examples.subject: Re: Using HINT Control to pull advanced metrics
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi Wyatt, 

Thanks so much for reaching out!

I'll include a document for you to use to test the signal on the G4SE which should help me lock down the issue you are facing. 

Outside of this, could you provide me with the following info? 

What is the address where the setup will be done? (For double checking the cell tower maps.)

What are your current data speeds, with and without the antenna?

How would you describe your internet usage? (e.g., streaming, working from home, gaming, etc.)

As for locating the tower you are connecting to, it won't be quite as simple as using the tower identifier on the app, as T-Mobile encrypts the cell ID. It's possible to convert it and check the tower, but honestly it's not worth really knowing, as the home internet setups will frequently switch to different towers based on the low priority of the setup and congestion. So what I normally suggest is optimizing the antenna based on metrics and positioning. 

Please let me know if you have any questions or need assistance with the app.

examples.subject: Re: Using HINT Control to pull advanced metrics
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi Wyatt, 

Thanks for all the info! 

I will include the map for you below, your Mom's location below. 

You can access this map here. I know you guys have seen this, just want to be thorough. 

Could you let me know the order number for the antenna? I didn't see anything linked to your email address. Just want to know which antenna you guys are using, as it will add a fair deal of context. 

So if I had to take a guess I would say you guys are connecting to the centre of the three towers, as we can see on the readings you are connecting to n41. Based on the same readings it looks like the issue is just distance and signal strength. n41 is definitely the frequency you are looking to connect to; the issue is just that high frequency towers don't have the range of low frequency ones. 

So just want to sync up on the antenna you guys are using and the direction you are aiming, we can work from there.

examples.subject: Re: Issues with OmniRoam 4-in-1 Low-Profile Mobile Antenna
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi J,

Thank you for reaching out to us. I'd be more than happy to assist.

Apologies for the delay; we've been a bit shorthanded. 

Unfortunately, I wasn't able to find your credentials in our database. Can you please help me with your order number? If you purchased it from Amazon, please also let me know your phone number.

With mobile setups like yours, performance can vary quite a bit depending on location, surroundings, and tower conditions. It's difficult to pinpoint the issue because we can't specifically aim the antenna toward another tower for example. Have you tried placing it on a different side of the SUV to compare? Can you share the signal metrics as well? They should be under cell info on the dashboard after you login to the gateway.

Best,

Finance and Front

Mission: 

To lay out best practices, tips, tricks, and outlined processes for how Finance and E Commerce communicate using Front, our "omni channel" platform.

Finance Team Scope of Work

Note : If item contains both a drop shipped item and a warehouse stocked item please tag both teams.

Weigh in on matters relating to payments and payment methods
Approve Net30 terms requests/Tax Exempt Status
Assist with creating and providing invoices from Xero
Advise e commerce and fulfillment team re. chargeback requests
~~Dropshipping questions and ship time information~~

For All

examples.subject: Re: Chat follow-up for Branson Buchanan
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi Branson,

Thank you so much for reaching out,

I had a look at your conversation with our chatbot and saw you need some tech support. I would love to help you get your system back to optimal performance. Before we proceed, there are a few general questions that will help us move on to the next steps that I’ve listed below:

Which carrier are you experiencing issues with?

What is the address where the booster is deployed? (This helps us review the cell tower maps in your area.)

In the meantime, could you please advise what the light indicators on the booster are doing (color and behavior). 

Also, please be sure to check all the cabling and connections.

If you have any questions, please be sure to reach out.

Best,

To ensure we speak with customers in a “unified voice” and that the conversations we have follow a logical flow, where we can get all the information we need the first time round and get them to a solution quickly, in a friendly and professional manner.

examples.subject: Re: Contact Form
examples.cleanedReply:
Hi Brad,

Thanks so much for reaching out!

Yes, you can definitely return any cabling that you haven’t used.

About the cell signal booster, unfortunately, it won’t improve your gateway’s performance. MIMO antennas and cell boosters are different systems that work in their own ways; each is designed for a specific purpose.

That said, we do have a great selection of boosters for cell signals. If you’re looking for a single carrier booster, I’d suggest checking out the Cel-Fi G41, which uses the same tower locations we mentioned earlier.

If you have any questions, feel free to reach out anytime.

Best,

examples.cleanedReply:
Hi Will, 

Wayne taking over from Anthony. 

Thanks so much for the info, including the map below for you with your location circled. 

You can access this map here. 

I would say my best recommendation for an antenna position would be facing due east from your location toward the two high frequency n41 towers. 

It's a bit of a tricky setup with the FX4100 as we do not have a guide for the unit, but I can confirm you have the correct antenna for the job! 

Let me know how the testing goes and if you need any help.

Info Gathering for Design

Once the project is qualified, we've built a strong rapport with the customer and we've got signal readings, we're almost ready to begin with the design. The next step is intensive info gathering, to ensure our design team has everything they need to draw up a good design for you. 

Here's how we go about info gathering 

1. Schedule a call with the customer to discuss the finer details that will be relevant to our design. 
Zoom works well, and 30 minutes is usually a good time estimate.
Provide them with a brief summary of the type of info you'll be asking them abou
